content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 3 fix-now issues in the nearby-work implementation: a blocking `br list` regression, a fail-open stale-data path for `br show` failures, and unbounded subprocess fan-out for highly connected beads.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `project create-from-bead` now aborts on any non-corrupt `br list` failure instead of falling back to the existing relation-only prompt, so a transient `br`/issues export problem blocks project creation entirely. `src/cli/project.rs:419-430` switched `load_bead_summaries` to `?`, and `src/cli/project.rs:2160-2173` maps ordinary `br list` exits into `AppError::Io`, so the new nearby-work path turns recoverable summary-loading failures into a hard stop. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:419-430`, `src/cli/project.rs:2160-2173`. remediation_hint: restore the old degraded-path behavior for summary loading, or at least degrade only the nearby-work section when `br list` is temporarily unavailable.
   Rationale: This is a user-visible regression on the main `create-from-bead` workflow: the branch now refuses to create a project in cases that previously succeeded with reduced context.
2. finding_summary: Nearby-work detail refresh silently discards every `br show` failure and then renders from stale summary/relation data as if the refresh succeeded, which can reintroduce closed/deferred beads or outdated titles/descriptions into `Nearby work`. `src/cli/project.rs:2133-2156` converts each `load_bead_detail` error to `None`, while `src/cli/project.rs:2776-2837` and `src/cli/project.rs:3171-3203` fall back to summary/relation status whenever detail is missing. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:2133-2156`, `src/cli/project.rs:2776-2837`, `src/cli/project.rs:3171-3203`. remediation_hint: propagate nearby detail-load failures, or conservatively omit entries whose authoritative detail could not be loaded instead of silently trusting stale summary data.
   Rationale: The whole point of the new per-bead refresh is to keep nearby context accurate enough for planned-elsewhere routing. Failing open here can mislead reviewers and implementers about what work is still active.
3. finding_summary: Nearby-work hydration now fans out one unbounded `br show` call per direct dependency/dependent before any production cap is applied, so highly connected beads can spawn dozens or hundreds of subprocesses during project creation. `src/cli/project.rs:1875-1886` feeds all IDs from `nearby_all_direct_relation_detail_candidate_ids`, `src/cli/project.rs:3076-3090` has no runtime limit, and `src/cli/project.rs:2133-2150` launches them all concurrently via `FuturesUnordered`. affected_files: `src/cli/project.rs:1875-1886`, `src/cli/project.rs:2133-2150`, `src/cli/project.rs:3076-3090`. remediation_hint: enforce a real runtime cap and/or semaphore around per-bead detail fetches, and prefer summary data for noncritical candidates.
   Rationale: This is a robustness problem on large bead graphs: the branch can overwhelm the local process runner or hit OS limits while trying to build prompt context.
